name: Gijón
notes: Two breakwaters, Fl.G/Fl.R. Fuel dock on starboard.

name: Avilés
notes: River entrance from N. Follow leading lights. Strong current on ebb.

name: Luarca
notes: Enter between breakwaters Fl.G/Fl.R. Visitor pontoon to port.

name: Ribadeo
notes: Enter ría between Illa Pancha and mainland.

name: Viveiro
notes: Deep ría. Marina Celeiro on E side.

name: Ferrol
notes: Narrow ría entrance between two castles. VHF 12.

name: Sada
notes: Enter Ría de Betanzos from NW.

name: La Coruña
notes: Inner harbor S side. Pontoons A-H. VHF 12. | Largest marina on route.  ◇  Yacht club. Slightly cheaper.

name: Camarinas
notes: Inside Ria de Camarinas, S of Cabo Vilan. Two channels: Canal de las Quebrantas (N - keep clear of Bajo Bufardo at Cabo Vilan; hermitage on 135 then 179 with Muxia church, pass between Farelo and Quebranta Chica) or Canal de A Barca (S, wider/easier). Call VHF 09. Some ground swell in strong W. | low-moderate; well sheltered inside ria | Only year-round marina on the Costa da Morte (Blue Flag). Essential refuge between La Coruña and Fisterra. Sailing school.

name: Muxia
notes: S shore of Ria de Camarinas. Canal A Barca (S, wider - Punta Villueira on 082 then 179 with Muxia church) or Canal Quebrantas (N - avoid Bajo Bufardo, 135 to hermitage then 179). Hazards: Las Quebrantas and Leixon de Juanboy shoals. Limited fuel-dock manoeuvring room. | low; well sheltered | Modern marina (2018), Blue Flag; one of the best-equipped on Costa da Morte. Camino waypoint.

name: Fisterra
notes: Small fishing harbour on the S side of Cabo Fisterra. Protected by main + SE breakwaters. Approach from NE after rounding the cape - keep well off (above-water rocks, kelp). In NW conditions prefer anchoring in Ria de Corcubion to the S. Inner harbour fills with fishing boats. Swell rolls in during southerlies. | moderate - southerly swell uncomfortable | Major psychological waypoint ('Land's End'). Fishing-harbour character, not full-service. Ria de Corcubion is a better refuge in most conditio

name: Muros
region: Galicia / Ria de Muros e Noia
notes: Ria de Muros ~3 nm wide at the mouth - approach unproblematic day or night. NE course; after Cabo Rebordino light turn into Ensenada de Muros. Marina behind breakwater in outer basin. Call VHF 09. Watch lobster pots near N entrance; NE swell brings short chop. | low-moderate; NE chop | Gated modern marina in a striking medieval town. ASNAUGA discount.

name: Portosin
region: Galicia / Ria de Muros e Noia
notes: S side of Ria de Muros e Noia. Mid-channel, avoid Quiebra island; 160 m canal between N end of fishing pier and dock light to E. Inner depth 6.4 m; mouth 8.5 m. Blue Flag. | low; well sheltered | Widely regarded the finest marina in Ria de Muros. 34 t travelift; English-speaking staff. 25% CA/ICC/RCC/OCC discount.

name: Ribeira
notes: SW corner of Ria de Arousa, N of Punta Cabeiro. Mouth 5 m, inner 2 m - shallow for deeper draught. Entrance 140 m wide. Watch Rua islet (mid-ria, small lighthouse) - navigate the channel S of it. Heavy fishing traffic. | check tide for >2.0 m draft | low | Busy fishing port; small yacht-club marina (local boats <=14 m). 40 t travelift.

name: Aguino
notes: NW entrance to Ria de Arousa. 300 m breakwater. Access via N channel between Isla de Salvora and mainland - identify Forcadiñas stones, Arbelas rocks, Meixon Vigo shoal. Primarily fishing; yacht access only in settled weather. Canal de Sagres offshore is challenging (250 m wide, 7-10 m) - calm + good charts only. | moderate swell only; reef hazards | moderate - exposed NW headland | Emergency refuge only - fishing port, no yacht marina.

name: A Pobra do Caraminal
notes: N coast of Ria de Arousa between Punta de Ladiña and Punta de la Merced. Main channel S of Jidoiro Pedregoso and Rua islet (1600 m wide, 60-70 m deep). Inner 3 m, entrance 5 m. Watch mussel rafts (bateas) throughout Arousa. | low | Well-sheltered marina in a pleasant town. 40 t travelift. Muport sister marina here has fuel. Bus to Santiago.

name: Vilagarcia de Arousa
notes: SE corner of Ria de Arousa via main channel - beware numerous UNLIT mussel rafts (bateas), alert navigation at night/fog. Entrance 25 m wide, 5 m at entrance, 3 m inner. Integrated in commercial port. VHF 09. | check depth for >2.5 m draft at MLW | low | Largest marina in the ria; rail link makes it an excellent crew-change port. 50 t travelift. ASNAUGA.

name: Vilanova de Arousa
notes: Mid-ria at Vilanova de Arousa via main channel avoiding bateas. Entrance 50 m wide, 5 m at mouth, 3 m inside. Mussel-raft anchor chains hang vertically - can foul props if passing close. | low | Recently built marina; 35 t travelift, good workshop; quieter alternative to Vilagarcia.

name: O Grove
notes: Porto Pedras Negras at the tip of the O Grove peninsula. Approach from E via marked channel - tricky in strong wind. Entrance 90 m, mouth 5 m, inner 2 m - shallow; keeled yachts near HW. Avoid O Grove shoals W of peninsula. 32 t travelift. | HW for yachts drawing >1.5 m | low | Base for O Grove peninsula and Illa de Ons day trip. Small-medium yachts only (14 m, 2 m draft).

name: Sanxenxo / Portonovo
notes: N shore of Ria de Pontevedra. Approach from W between Illa de Ons/Onza (swell protection) and mainland. Punta Cabicastro (44 m monolith) key landmark; Helmo rock 100 m S of it - give clearance. Entrance 70 m, mouth 7.5 m, inner 3.5 m. | low-moderate; Ons gives protection but strong SW swell can enter | Largest marina in Ria de Pontevedra (to 45 m). 64 t travelift. Very busy Jul/Aug.  ◇  1 nm W of Sanxenxo; same ria approach (clear of Helmo rock S of Cabicastro). Entrance 170 m, mouth 8 m, inner

name: Combarro
notes: Upper Ria de Pontevedra, N of Pontevedra city. Outer pontoon 3 m MLW (charts often show shallower - marina dredged). Approach from S mid-channel; old chart depths misleading. Anchor off outer pontoon in ~6 m at HW if full. | outer pontoon 3 m MLW - sufficient for most cruisers | low; well up inside ria | 5-star marina, 5 TYHA Gold Anchors - one of Spain's finest small marinas. 50 t travelift. Highly recommended.

name: Pontevedra
notes: Mouth of the Rio Lerez at the head of the ria. Severely limited: max draft 2 m at LW (spring range ~3 m); Barca bridge blocks sailing vessels over 12 m air draft. Not suitable for most offshore cruising yachts. | HW only; tidal gate | low | Impractical for most cruising yachts (bridge air draft + shallow LW). Combarro is a far better option; visit Pontevedra by dinghy/bus.

name: Marin
notes: Large mixed military/commercial/fishing port at the head of Ria de Pontevedra (Spanish Naval Academy). Not a recreational marina - contact port authority VHF 16; temporary fishing-quay berth not routine. | low | NOT a recreational marina. Navy/commercial/fishing. Use Combarro or Sanxenxo instead.

name: Bueu
notes: S shore of Ria de Pontevedra at the entrance to Ria de Aldan. Approach from main channel - straightforward. Busy mussel fleet; sports area separate from fishing quays. | low-moderate; some SW swell at mouth | Useful stop between Pontevedra/Sanxenxo and Ria de Vigo; gateway to Ria de Aldan. Limited capacity (100 berths).

name: Cangas
notes: N shore of Ria de Vigo between Cabo Balea and Punta Rodeira. Day: enter S (main) channel on Cabo Stay leading line 069; then 041 on Borneira tower/Rodeira/Domaio; leave red Borneira buoy to port; NE to breakwater. Night: Cies then Cabo Silleiro; Boeiro buoy; 069 then 040. | low inside ria; entry channel exposed to occasional SW | Excellent well-run marina; 64 t travelift; ferry to Vigo for crew changes; access to Cies (7 nm).

name: Moana Mar
notes: N shore of Ria de Vigo, E of Cangas. From mid-channel, when Vigo Maritime Station bears ahead (S) steer for the Moana breakwater tip. Fishing port to W, sports marina to E. | low; well inside ria | Quiet town-centre marina; good value (~EUR20/night to 12 m); very well protected.

name: Vigo
notes: Bouzas, just E of Davila Sport. Approach from W. Entrance 3 m; max vessel 12 m. 8 t crane; 48 buoy berths plus pontoons. Blue Flag. | low; inside ria | Blue Flag sailing club; small-medium yachts (max 12 m); budget-friendlier than Davila.  ◇  City-centre, S shore of Ria de Vigo. Approach from W via main channel. Mouth 28 m wide, 10 m at mouth but only 2 m at innermost berths - critical; best berths 4-8 m. 32 t travelift. | inner harbour 2 m min - check draft | low; fully inside ria | Most centra

name: Baiona
notes: Adjacent to MRCYB, same bay. Entrance ~083 (leading line with Punta Vicos on Cies and Cabo Silleiro 1.3 nm astern). Approach from SW/S. 12 m entrance, 6 m inner. VHF 09. 17 t travelift. | low-moderate | Municipal marina adjacent to MRCYB; often has space when MRCYB is full. Max 40 m / 6 m draft; fuel dock.  ◇  SW shore of Ria de Vigo, 4 nm SSW of Islas Cies. Approach from S/NW round Cabo Silleiro (keep 1+ nm off large rocks); NW with Monte Real castle as landmark; enter between breakwaters ~083

name: A Guarda
notes: Open cove facing NW (400x100 m). Landmark Monte Santa Tecla. Entrance alignment beacons on 109; entrance width 8.2 m - VERY NARROW. Fog signal. Deepens to 8 m at breakwater head, 0 m at slipway. Three exposed rocks off Punta Jinete (N shore). DANGEROUS in W/SW - do not attempt entry. Fishing harbour. | daylight, slack, settled weather only | poor - exposed W/NW; dangerous in bad weather | ONLY for small craft in settled weather; 8.2 m entrance is very tight; emergency use only for cruising yacht

name: Caminha
notes: Minho-mouth bar ~1400 m, exposed to W/SW swell. Approach via Barra Sul between Insua islet and Ponta Ruiva. No charted channel marks - local knowledge strongly recommended. Max draft 2.5 m; tidal range ~2 m springs. Capitania signal station near Ponta do Cabedelo issues mandatory instructions. Ongoing silting; bar shifts seasonally. PT/ES border on the river centreline. | Near HW, with local knowledge/pilot | high - W/SW swell makes the bar dangerous | Northernmost Portuguese refuge, just inside

name: Viana do Castelo
notes: River Lima entrance. Keep outside the 20 m contour from N or S to avoid rocky ledges. Entrance dredged to 8 m - passable all weathers/tides. At night use rear leading light on 005. Bar builds between outer-mole end and first port-hand buoy; in heavy weather keep 200 m off the breakwater end and enter from S. Spring ebb + river outflow tricky; obstruction ~2.5 m below LWS - deep-draught wait for tide. Channel buoyed inside; pass fishing harbour to port to the Eiffel-design steel bridge. | Flood p

name: Povoa de Varzim
notes: Harbour entered on 045. From N give the N breakwater wide clearance - waves break at its extremity in any swell. Green buoys mark shallows near the breakwater. 21 nm S of Viana, 14 nm N of Leixoes. Depths 1.6-3.0 m inside; 4 visitor berths have min 3 m. Exposed breakwater head dangerous in heavy NW swell. | moderate - NW swell breaks at breakwater head | Busy summer resort marina; adequate overnight between Viana and Leixoes; prefer Leixoes in bad weather.

name: Leixoes
notes: Inside the large commercial harbour of Leixoes, 14 nm S of Povoa, ~2 nm N of the Douro. Outer commercial port entered in any conditions - big breakwaters give full protection. In W winds, strong swell + spray at the entrance. HEAVY commercial ship traffic - monitor VHF 16, keep clear. Proceed past the oil jetty to starboard; reception pontoon to port on entry. 5.5 t crane. | low - well inside commercial port | Best all-weather port for Porto crew changes - can be entered in all conditions. Run b

name: Vila Nova de Gaia
region: Portugal / Norte (Vila Nova de Gaia / Porto)
notes: The Douro bar is potentially the most dangerous entrance on this coast in adverse conditions. N side protected by a long breakwater (red/white banded light). From S pass along the detached W breakwater, 100 m off until the entrance. Stay in the marked fairway - sandbanks to starboard. W wind + ebb + river outflow = steep breaking seas; port may close. Call marina VHF 09 before entry; enter near HW. Marina 1.5 nm upriver on the S bank. | Near HW (any swell/wind); call VHF 09 | high at bar - estua

name: Aveiro
notes: WARNING: the Aveiro Barra is one of Portugal's most dangerous river-bar entrances. Channel between the W breakwater and an E dike, oriented 335, heads ~550 m apart; follow the W pier ~50 m off. Strong tidal currents; marina entrance narrow (~20 m). In any onshore wind/swell, especially on the ebb, the bar produces dangerous breaking seas and can close. Do NOT enter before half-flood, daylight only. Barra bridge 14 m clearance at HW. Aveiro harbour control VHF 11 broadcasts bar conditions. Ria de

name: Figueira da Foz
notes: WARNING: dangerous bar - has caused groundings and fatalities. Entrance dredged to min 7 m but swell builds in any onshore wind; in strong onshore gales huge standing waves form and the port closes. Enter on 073 between breakwaters. Arrive in the last 2 h of rising tide (flood counteracts river outflow). On the ebb (esp springs) cross-currents/eddies at marina entrance; spring ebb to 6 kt in winter. Bar conditions on VHF 11; entry permission VHF 16; berthing VHF 09. Large commercial fishing port

name: Nazare
notes: Unusually, Nazare can be entered at virtually any tide and in almost any weather - the basin is protected by its configuration. From N: dramatic Sitio promontory (105 m) 1 nm N of the harbour. The Nazare Canyon amplifies long-period NW swell (world-record big waves at Praia do Norte, 1 nm N) - keep well clear of Praia do Norte. In big NW swells visitor pontoons may surge. Facility reportedly run-down in recent reports. | Any - no tidal gate | moderate - basin protected but big NW swell surges; P

name: Peniche
notes: Peniche peninsula; marina (Ribeira basin) inside the W jetty. Fair approach, good channel marking, negligible current/tidal flow in the basin. Max draft 4 m (chart verify). Pump-out, crane, ramp. Fishing-vessel wash at the entrance. The Berlengas (nature reserve, dangerous rocks) lie 6-10 nm W - ensure well clear on the approach. | low - good protection | Best jumping-off point for the Berlengas. Good shelter, 20 visitor berths, laundry. World-class surf (Supertubos). 15 m LOA limit restricts la

name: Cascais
notes: N shore of Cascais Bay, 9 nm SE of Cabo da Roca. From N stay 1 nm off Cabo da Roca and Cabo Raso. Leave the three S cardinal buoys and a red can to port along the S breakwater. Entrance runs NE-SW; reception dock on N side. Min depth 6 m. External pontoon N of breakwater takes 50 m LOA. VHF 09. Approach boisterous in strong W-SW - arrive before conditions deteriorate. | low-moderate - protected in bay; approach rough in strong W-SW | 'Home of international sailing in Portugal'. 70 t travel hoist

name: Oeiras
notes: Mouth of the Tagus, 0.5 nm E of Sao Juliao da Barra fortress (distinctive tower landmark). Approach from SW following the S bank of the Tagus. No significant bar/swell - the Tagus mouth shelters from the Atlantic. Range 14 nm. Diesel + 95 unleaded; 100 dry-storage; pump-out. | low - sheltered inside Tagus mouth | Excellent all-round marina 8 nm up the Tagus, 15 nm from central Lisbon; more sheltered/quieter than Cascais.

name: Lisboa (Tejo)
notes: Easternmost/furthest-upriver Lisbon marina. VHF 09/16. Galp fuel on site. Double-sluice lock isolates vessels from Tagus tidal range (constant level). Max draft 2.4 m (RESTRICTIVE - deeper vessels use Alcantara/Cascais). Max LOA 25 m (river pier to ~230 m for superyachts). Travel lift, crane, nautical shop. | very low - deep inside Tagus | Best crew-change marina in Lisbon (5 min from airport, walk to Oriente rail). Galp fuel. 2.4 m draft limit restricts deeper keels. Largest Lisbon-area marina

name: Sesimbra
notes: Marina at the SE end of the fishing-harbour breakwater. Approach straightforward once the long SE breakwater is identified; give the breakwater end clearance (fishing boats exit fast). Call VHF 09/12 (16 calling). Fuel is in the fishing harbour (not the marina). Good anchorage in sand off the beach, open E-SE; bay sheltered from prevailing NW by Serra da Arrabida cliffs; ~70 summer mooring buoys. | low-moderate - sheltered from NW; SE swell enters bay | One of the most attractive stops on the ro

name: Setubal
notes: Head of the Sado estuary, 40 km S of Lisbon by sea. Enter between the Troia Peninsula (N) and Outao headland (S). Approach buoy No.2 then ~040 for 3 nm toward Outao fort. At night Doca and Azeda lights form a leading line. Watch sandbanks on the Troia (NW) side. Resident bottlenose dolphins in the Sado. Marina split E (170 berths, <=10 m, 2.5 m) and W (150 berths, <=15 m, 2.5 m). Ferry pier opposite - beware wash/traffic. VHF 16. | low - inside Sado estuary | Gateway to Arrabida Natural Park (li

name: Sines
notes: Marina entrance 1.5 nm S of Cabo de Sines; a 1700 m N-S breakwater protects it. From N beware a submerged outer breakwater section marked by a red buoy - do NOT pass inside it. Leave the red buoy to port. Dominant NW summer winds; winter strong S-SW. Some early-morning fog Jul-Oct. Deep anchorage area inside the breakwater. 45 t travel lift; 5 t crane. Customs available. | low - long breakwater; S swell may surge in winter | Primary staging post for Atlantic/Med passages - best shelter between S

name: Sagres (Baleeira)
notes: Harbour entrance at ~37 00.7N, 8 55.4W (confirm on chart). Small commercial fishing harbour with seawall offering shelter. Free anchorage to W of seawall end or among fishing boats. Can be gusty in afternoons. No designated visitor berths; used as passage stop only. Open to S. | moderate - seawall provides partial shelter but open to S | Operational fishing harbour managed by Docapesca. Used as overnight stop before or after rounding Cabo de Sao Vicente. Access to Sagres Fortress and town. Ancho

name: Lagos
notes: Approach on bearing 282 between west harbour mole head and tower of Santo Antonio church. Follow channel to waiting pontoon to starboard, immediately before the lifting bridge. Request bridge opening on VHF 09 - not available during 10-min window before daily train departures. Basin dredged to -2.80 m; channel to minimum 3.0 m. Fuel pontoon (diesel + unleaded 95) on starboard near bridge. | very low - fully enclosed | One of the 'gateway' marinas to the Algarve; designated customs clearance port

name: Portimao
notes: Enter from due S between two long moles (~250 m wide entrance). Leading line: align Ferragudo fore and aft beacons, then course 020 - leave buoy No.2 to port, continue to buoy No.4, then bear to marina entrance. Bar entrance depth 8.5 m; quay depth 4 m. Deep-water access at all tides. Reception pontoon to starboard. Full boatyard (Ferragudo, E bank) with 50 t and 300 t travel lifts. | very low - enclosed river estuary | Designated customs clearance port. Deepest draft access in the Algarve (5 m)

name: Albufeira
notes: Approach from E, entering outer basin between two breakwaters on bearing ~315. Light structures: red/white (port) and green/white (starboard) on breakwater ends. Channel dredged to 4.0 m. Inner basin from outer basin; finger pontoons 2.5-4.0 m. Described as the most sheltered marina in Portugal. | low - enclosed outer and inner basins | 15 nm E of Portimao, 7 nm W of Vilamoura. Boatyard: 70 t travel lift, 6 t crane, repair booths. TYHA 5 Gold Anchors, Blue Flag. Book ahead in summer.

name: Vilamoura
notes: Call 'Vilamoura Radio' VHF 09 (24h) or Ch 16. Marina control tower beacon 18 m (range 19 nm). E entry beacon green/white, W entry beacon red/white (5 nm). W mole 660 m, E mole 430 m. Entry depth 4.0 m; inner basin 3.3 m and 2.0 m. Vessels >35 m LOA / >3 m draft call ahead. Reception quay and fuel station in inner channel. | low - enclosed basin | Largest marina in Portugal; designated customs/immigration clearance port (SEF). 'Best Marina in Portugal' repeatedly. Very busy Jul-Aug; quiet Oct-May

name: Faro
notes: Via Ria Formosa inner channels from Barra do Farol. Follow channel NW to buoy 23, then anchor or moor in Doca Recreio; dinghy to far wall. Strong currents 3-5 kt at springs. Long fetch in channel when windy at HW. Primarily naval/yacht-club facility. A larger Porto de Recreio de Faro (~277 vessels) was in planning as of 2024. | Rising tide; arrive near slack before flood | very low - lagoon channel | Anchor or moor to explore Faro city and use the airport. Mostly club berths - visitor space limi

name: Olhao
notes: Access via Ria Formosa inner channels after Barra do Farol. Follow buoyed Olhao channel NW. Navigable 365 days. Depth at MLW 2-3 m alongside. MAX LOA 17 m is a critical limitation. Significant tidal flow. Travel lift for haulout. | Via Barra do Farol on flood | very low - fully enclosed lagoon | Inside Ria Formosa Natural Park. 17 m max length significantly restricts larger yachts - confirm fit before planning. Mechanical workshop on site.

name: Vila Real de Santo Antonio
notes: Rio Guadiana entrance ~12 nm E of Tavira; 600 m mole on W bank marks entrance; channel buoyed ~1 nm offshore. Bar waypoints (2 m draft at LW neaps): 37 09.686N 7 23.829W; 37 09.268N 7 23.641W; 37 08.724N 7 23.693W. STRONG cross-current at entrance - dangerous other than near slack. Ebb 2-4 kt, flood 1-2 kt. Marina ~2 nm upriver on W bank, S of ferry dock. Accessible HW +/- 3 h. | Slack water at entrance; approach HW +/- 3 h | low inside river; entrance rough with onshore wind on ebb | Easternmos

name: Ayamonte
notes: Spanish bank of the Rio Guadiana at the PT/ES border. Guadiana bar shallow/variable - enter on second half of a rising tide. Entrance can fall to 2-3 m at MLW; bar dredged ~2016 but sandbanks shift after floods. Keep to the Portuguese (W) bank until well into the estuary, then turn to the Spanish side. Strong cross-current on the ebb; driftwood hazard. Port of Entry - Customs available. | HW +/- 2h (rising preferred) | moderate | Southernmost western Gulf-of-Cadiz stop; good refuge and Atlantic-

name: Isla Canela
notes: In the Carreras River (Cano de Isla Canela), ~650 m upriver of Punta del Caiman. Access channel min 50 m wide, min 3 m depth. Tidal streams up to 2 kt; enter on rising tide. Chart D-440. | Rising tide | moderate | Resort marina; quieter alternative to Ayamonte. Blue Flag.

name: Isla Cristina
notes: Enter Rio Carreras channel on ~312. Red/white landfall buoy half a mile offshore - leave close to starboard. Lit red/green mole-end markers. Keep to port side of buoyed channel to avoid shallows to starboard. Entrance ~3.0 m, marina ~2.5 m. Currents up to 2 kt on ebb; rising tide. Fishing/commercial traffic. | HW +/- 2h (rising) | moderate | Busy fishing port with sports marina. Travel lift 32 t, crane 5 t, hardstanding. Workmanlike haulout stop.

name: El Rompido
notes: Rio Piedras, 16 nm E of PT border. Landfall buoy ~37 11.58N 007 03.16W; river entrance buoyed. Bar reduces to ~1 m at MLW; HW depths 3.0-4.7 m. DO NOT attempt near LW or in onshore wind/swell. Rising tide near HW essential. Shifting sandbanks; buoys may lag after heavy weather. Marina ~6 nm upriver (dunes/wildlife). Staff assist mooring. | Near HW (+/- 1h) | high | Scenic tidal-river stop; shallow bar -> shoal-draft or HW only. 24h security, supermarket adjacent.

name: Punta Umbria
notes: Rio de Punta Umbria (Odiel tributary). Long mole with S cardinal at end protects W side of estuary. Channel buoyed from entrance. Two marinas: RCMT (~197 berths, <=12 m) ~1 nm in; Marina Umbria (~260 berths, 65 transit) a further 0.75 nm upriver. Entry near HW advised. Chart D-440. | Rising tide preferred | moderate | Popular local weekend marina; access to Donana NP. Berth count is aggregate; transit berths at Marina Umbria.

name: Mazagon
notes: At the Rio Odiel/Huelva entrance. Curving 3-nm mole extends the S bank ~1.5 nm seaward; prominent white pillar with red top at end. Head for the W cardinal buoy at the start of the buoyed channel; markers lead ~1 nm N to the marina. Training wall protects from S. Entrance ~4.5 m, marina 2.5-4.5 m. Well protected all directions. | low | Excellent well-protected marina; good for extended stay/maintenance. Travel lift 32 t. Yacht club with pool. Useful before/after the Donana area.

name: Chipiona
notes: Chipiona lighthouse (62 m, tallest in Spain) conspicuous ~1 nm SW of entrance. HAZARD: Bajo Salmedina reef extends ~2 nm offshore - marked by an E cardinal buoy; STAY SEAWARD of it before turning E for the harbour. Fairway buoy 1.3 nm SE of lighthouse. Visitors berth outer basin to port of entrance. Tidal range 3.5-3.7 m. Often full in high season - call ahead. | moderate | Gateway to the Guadalquivir; departure point for the river passage to Seville. Travel lift 45 t. Salmedina reef is a seriou

name: Rota
notes: 10 nm SSE of Chipiona, 5 nm NNW of Cadiz. After rounding the 'Corrales' fish traps (to 0.5 nm offshore), give Punta de la Morena a wide berth - shallows as little as 0.7 m within 0.5 nm. Adjacent to NATO Naval Station Rota - follow marked channel; military area to S. Max draft 2.5 m limits deeper keels. | low | Most convenient marina for the town of Rota. Shallow 2.5 m draft limit is restrictive. NATO base restricted zone to S.

name: El Puerto de Santa Maria
notes: Purpose-built marina 3 nm NE of Cadiz city, just N of the main channel to El Puerto de Santa Maria. Entrance channel 100 m wide, 200 m long. Bay of Cadiz tidal streams up to 2-3 kt at springs - time the approach. Well-marked. | low | Largest marina on the Bay of Cadiz; full resort facilities. Travelift 200 t (one of the largest on this coast) - excellent haulout.  ◇  On the Rio Guadalete, 5 nm NE of Cadiz city. Access via buoyed channel between breakwaters into the river. Strong current - care b

name: Cadiz
notes: N end of Cadiz city within the commercial port. Bay of Cadiz is well-marked deep water. Tidal streams 2-3 kt at springs in the main channel; follow port-authority directions. Small marina (max LOA 15 m). 10 t crane, slipway. | low | Compact urban marina inside Cadiz commercial port; 15 m LOA limit; 10 t crane (not a haulout). For larger yachts use RCN Cadiz or Puerto Sherry.  ◇  Within Cadiz Bay near the city waterfront. 160 finger berths with water/electricity. Bay of Cadiz well-marked; tidal s

name: Sancti Petri
notes: Inside the Sancti Petri channel (Cano de Sancti Petri), S of Chiclana. Entrance width 100 m; depth 2 m at LW; interior 0-12 m variable. Conspicuous Sancti Petri Castle on islet at channel entrance is the leading mark. Approach on rising tide - bar tricky at LW. | Rising tide (bar 2 m at LW) | moderate | Picturesque tidal-channel marina near Sancti Petri Castle ruins; good anchorage off the entrance; staging point before Barbate/Tarifa.

name: Barbate
notes: 36 10.8N 005 55.5W. Protected by 1300 m breakwater + floating anti-wave barrier at entrance. Barbate lighthouse (sectorized R/W). CAUTION: almadraba (tuna-trap) nets set Apr-Jun extend E-W near the breakwater E of the entrance - go well clear to seaward/W before turning in. Dredged channel 256 m. First visit: HW +/- 2h in light conditions due to shifting sand. Tarifa 20 nm SE (~2h motor). | moderate | THE key Strait-of-Gibraltar staging port eastbound: depart ~3h before HW Gibraltar for the favo

name: Tarifa
notes: Tarifa Island (connected by causeway); lighthouse at S tip conspicuous. Harbour 0.75 nm NNE of island tip. APBA manages 118 small-craft berths (5-11 m) for local/day-trip vessels. Yachts on passage: REFUGE ONLY, berth alongside central jetty if space. Heavy Tarifa-Tangier ferry traffic - give way. Levante can blow F8-9 funnelling through the Strait. Los Cabezos shoal breaks W of entrance even in calm; tunny nets off approaches esp. W. | poor | REFUGE/EMERGENCY ONLY for yachts - no fuel, no dedic

name: La Linea
notes: 36 09N 005 22W. Bay of Algeciras, W of Gibraltar. Excellent shelter all weather; deep-water access. Call VHF 09 before arrival. Keep clear of the Algeciras commercial shipping lane / TSS. La Linea border crossing to Gibraltar ~2 km (pedestrian); Gibraltar airport 2 km. Strait currents 4-7 kt at springs - time the tidal gate. | low | Largest, best-equipped marina in the region; ideal final Spanish stop before Gibraltar/Med. 75 t hoist - best haulout on the Strait. Fuel: diesel, 95-octane, Calor g

name: Gibraltar
notes: N of Gibraltar Harbour between the North Mole and the airport runway. Pre-arrival notification (ASYCUDA); Q-flag; VHF 71; customs via marina staff. Shares operator with Ocean Village (combined ~250+ berths). | low | Traditional marina feel; preferred by long-term cruisers over Ocean Village's entertainment focus. Duty-free diesel (Cepsa). Gibraltar is a non-Schengen UK Overseas Territory (May 2026).  ◇  W side of Gibraltar, outside the N end of the harbour, adjacent to the airport runway. Pre-ar
